Rebuilt batch processing with Spring Batch + Kafka. Chunk-oriented processing (1000 records per chunk) with checkpointing. Kafka topics for dead-letter queues — failed records retried automatically. Parallel step execution for independent job flows.

Disney's batch processing for invoicing and cinema payments was a nightly 6+ hour operation. If the batch failed at hour 4, the entire run restarted from zero — no checkpointing. Operations team was on-call every night during batch windows.

: Replaced the monolithic batch script with chunk-oriented processing. Each chunk processed 1,000 records with transactional integrity — failures only re-processed the failed chunk, not the entire batch.

: Records that failed business validation were sent to a Kafka DLQ topic. A retry consumer processed them with exponential backoff (1s, 5s, 30s, 2m, 10m) — transient failures self-healed without manual intervention.
: Independent job flows (invoicing vs cinema payments) ran in parallel — cutting wall-clock time.
: Custom Micrometer metrics exposed batch progress, throughput, and failure rates to Prometheus.
: Eliminated — DLQ + retry handles all transient failures
: Operations team no longer needed during batch windows
: Failed chunk restarts in seconds, not hours
Invoicing and cinema payment batch jobs took 6+ hours nightly. Failed batches delayed next-day operations. No retry mechanism — failures required manual intervention.
Co-developed batch processing infrastructure for invoicing and cinema payments
Batch window reduced from 6 hours to 1.2 hours. Processes 2M records per batch. Failed records auto-retry with exponential backoff — manual intervention eliminated.
